reasoning: The base rate of 3.167 protests per 30 days implies about 38 protests over the 360-day window from March 2025 to Feb 2026. If these were uniformly distributed, a Poisson distribution gives a ~39% chance of seeing 4 or more protests in the target 30-day period (which is required to resolve YES). However, it is highly likely that these protests were clustered, perhaps around the water-related unrest seen in late March 2025. Given that targeted web searches found absolutely no reports of protests, strikes, or civil unrest in Martinique during January or February 2026, the current underlying rate of protests is very likely lower than the annual average. Without an active wave of unrest, the probability of hitting 4 or more protests in the single month leading up to March 8, 2026, is well below the Poisson generic expectation. I estimate this at 18%.
